title: Kubernetes Audit Logging Levels
description:
## Kubernetes Audit Logging

You are writing an audit policy for the kube-apiserver. You need to capture the **complete request AND response body** for all write operations on `secrets` in any namespace, so you have a full record of every secret modification.

Which audit level must you specify for these events?

options.text: `None` — discard all events matching this rule; nothing is logged
`Metadata` — logs request metadata (user, verb, resource, timestamp) but not the request or response body
`Request` — logs metadata and the request body, but not the response body
`RequestResponse` — logs metadata, the full request body, and the full response body

explanation: Kubernetes audit levels in ascending verbosity: **None → Metadata → Request → RequestResponse**. `RequestResponse` is required when you need the complete picture — what was sent and what the API server returned. This is most important for sensitive resources like Secrets. The audit policy file is passed to kube-apiserver via `--audit-policy-file`. Note that `RequestResponse` is expensive; use `Metadata` for most resources.

hints.title: Audit policy levels
hints.body: The four levels: `None` (discard), `Metadata` (headers only), `Request` (+ request body), `RequestResponse` (+ response body). Configure via `--audit-policy-file` and `--audit-log-path` on the kube-apiserver.
hints.command:
# Example rule targeting Secrets at RequestResponse level:
# - level: RequestResponse
#   resources:
#   - group: ""
#     resources: ["secrets"]
